title: Understanding Angular Acceleration
text: Angular acceleration is the rate of change of angular velocity over time. It can be calculated using the formula: angular acceleration $$=$$ change in angular velocity / time.

title: Change in Angular Velocity
text: The change in angular velocity is the final angular velocity minus the initial angular velocity. Since the Earth started from rest, the initial angular velocity is $$0$$.

title: Final Angular Velocity
text: The final angular velocity is given as $$1$$ revolution per day. Convert this to radians per second for calculation purposes.

title: Convert Revolutions to Radians
text: $$1$$ revolution is equal to $$2\pi$$ radians. Therefore, $$1$$ revolution per day is equal to $$2\pi$$ radians per day. Convert this to radians per second.

title: Calculate Angular Acceleration
text: Use the formula for angular acceleration: angular acceleration $$=$$ change in angular velocity / time. Use the converted angular velocity and the time period of $$6$$ days.
